news.summary: Wolverine World Wide, Inc. shared its financial outcomes for the quarter ended on March 29, 2025. Chris Hufnagel, President and CEO of Wolverine Worldwide, expressed satisfaction with the results, attributing them to the company's strategy and team's performance. The first quarter saw significant growth, with Merrell and Saucony driving double-digit revenue rises. Earnings also saw impressive growth, tripling in comparison to the previous year. The company's stock is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol WWW.
news.headline: Wolverine Worldwide (NYSE: WWW) reports Q1 2025 results with tripled earnings, Merrell & Saucony seeing double-digit revenue growth.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures saw an increase after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates steady at 4.25% to 4.5%. The tariff increases have surpassed expectations, leading to concerns about inflation, weaker growth, and unemployment. The 10-year Treasury bond yield was 4.31%, while the two-year bond was at 3.82%. Market projections show a 79.8% likelihood of rates staying unchanged in June. Nasdaq 100 futures rose by 1.38%, S&P 500 by 0.94%, and Dow Jones by 0.72%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF rose in premarket. S&P 500, Dow Jones, and Russell 2000 indices also showed positive performance. Uber reported revenue below estimates, while Disney had favorable results. U.S. mortgage applications rose by 11% for the week ending May 2nd. Nasdaq Composite was up by 0.27%, S&P 500 by 0.43%, Dow Jones by 0.70%, and Russell 2000 by 0.33%. Jurrien Timmer, Director, shared insights on a 10-year S&P 500 chart.
news.headline: US stocks likely to open higher after Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady at 4.25%-4.5%. Futures show increases in Dow Jones (+0.72%), S&P 500 (+0.94%), Nasdaq 100 (+1.38%), and Russell 2000 (+1.31%). Bond yields: 10-year Treasury 4.31%, two-year bond 3.82%. Positive movement in ETFs tracking S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100. S&P 500 Index up 1.14%, Nasdaq 100 up 1.51% in premarket. Top sectors in S&P 500: health care, consumer discretionary, and information technology. Uber reports low first-quarter revenue, Disney sees positive fiscal Q2 results. US mortgage applications up 11% from prior week.
First Advantage releases Q1 2025 performance metrics.

news.summary: On May 8, 2025, Sapiens International Corporation, a prominent provider of insurance industry software, disclosed its financial outcomes for Q1 ending March 31, 2025. The company, traded as NASDAQ: SPNS and TASE: SPNS, reported a series of results for the quarter.

news.summary: Canadian Tire Corporation, Limited announced its first-quarter results for 2025, with a 4.7% growth in consolidated comparable sales and 4.0% increase in retail revenue. Q1 saw a rise in both essential and discretionary purchasing, driving increased customer traffic. Q1 Normalized Diluted Earnings Per Share (EPS) stood at $2.18, up $0.80, while Diluted EPS was $0.67, down $0.71 compared to the prior year. The company introduced its transformative growth strategy, True North, aiming to increase shareholder value with over $2 billion in capital investments over four years. The strategy includes expanding Triangle Rewards, new store concepts, and more personalized customer relationships. During Q1, Canadian Tire Retail (CTR) saw a 4.7% growth, SportChek had a 6.3% rise in comparable sales, and Mark's experienced a 2.2% growth. Retail segment revenue was $3,061.8 million, up 4.0% from last year, with Retail IBT at $50.9 million. Financial Services segment IBT was $97.0 million, while CT REIT saw a 3.9% increase in diluted Adjusted Funds from Operations per unit. Total capital expenditures were $115.9 million. The company is progressing towards divestiture of the Helly Hansen business, aiming for closure before the end of Q2 to unlock capital for shareholders and strategic investments.
news.headline: Canadian Tire Corp reported Q1 2025 results with 4.7% comp sales growth, $3.46B revenue, $2B investment in True North strategy, and $51.6M IBT.

news.summary: Bitcoin's realized cap is approaching $900 billion, setting new records and indicating a potential price breakout. Data from CryptoQuant shows growing conviction in Bitcoin's value, with the realized cap hitting $891 billion on May 7. The influx of capital into Bitcoin reflects renewed investor interest, potentially signaling the early stages of a new bull cycle. Concerns remain over profit-taking affecting the market's sustainability, but Glassnode suggests buy and sell-side conditions are balanced at around $100,000. Capital inflows have consistently exceeded outflows since October 2023, indicating a sustained interest in Bitcoin's potential. This article does not provide investment advice, and readers are encouraged to conduct their own research before making decisions.
news.headline: Bitcoin nearing $100K, with realized cap close to $900B. Potential for significant price surge seen; market bullish. Profit-taking not hindering rebound. BTC setting new network value highs, indicating possible start of new bull cycle.

news.summary: Increasingly high demand for electricity, rural electrification initiatives, grid modernization projects, and the integration of renewable energy sources are fueling growth in the electrical power pole market. Despite these factors, the market faces constraints such as the substantial installation expenses, regulatory obstacles, and concerns regarding the environmental effects of wooden poles. Nonetheless, the market continues to advance steadily.
news.headline: Global electrical power pole market expected to reach $21.9 billion by 2034 with 5.5% CAGR growth, driven by increasing electricity demand and renewable energy integration. Challenges include high installation costs and regulatory obstacles.

news.summary: In Toronto, the World Ovarian Cancer Coalition has launched the Global Expert Advisory Group on Ovarian Cancer on World Ovarian Cancer Day (#WOCD2025). This initiative aims to raise awareness about ovarian cancer as a crucial global health concern. Ovarian cancer, the deadliest of female cancers, lacks a dependable screening test and faces substantial diagnostic challenges, putting countless lives at risk. This issue is even more pressing in low- and middle-income nations, where a significant number of individuals are affected.
news.headline: World Ovarian Cancer Coalition establishes Global Expert Advisory Group to combat ovarian cancer, a leading cause of female cancer deaths. #WOCD2025.

news.summary: During the recent May Day holiday in Hong Kong, there was a substantial increase in orders from supermarkets and department stores, more than 1.7 times higher than usual. This spike was driven by a surge in domestic travel and consumer spending during the holiday period. Instant delivery service platforms experienced higher demand due to strong consumer interest.
news.headline: SF Intra-city delivery orders rose by 87% during May Day holiday. Supermarket and department store orders surged by over 1.7 times.

news.headline: Pakistan hosts DCO's DFDI Forum, with 700 leaders attending. Pakistan initiates Digital FDI Initiative with $700M investment.

news.summary: Today, Emera, listed on the TSX as EMA, released its first quarter financial report for 2025 from Halifax, Nova Scotia. The company showcased strong performance with impressive growth numbers. The adjusted earnings per share (EPS) for the quarter rose by 68% to $1.28 compared to $0.76 in the first quarter of the previous year. The reported EPS for Q1 in 2025 increased to $1.96 from $0.73 in Q1 2024, mainly attributed to market-to-market (“MTM”) gains recorded this year compared to MTM losses in the previous year.
news.headline: Emera (TSX: EMA) reports strong Q1 2025 financial results - EPS grew 68% to $1.28, reported EPS increased to $1.96.

news.summary: Prestige Consumer Healthcare Inc. reported Q4 revenue of $296.5 million, a 7.0% increase from the previous year, with organic revenue growing 7.9%. Fiscal 2025 revenue was $1,137.8 million, up 1.1%, with diluted EPS of $4.29 and adjusted EPS of $4.52. Leverage reduced to 2.4x. Initial fiscal 2026 outlook projects 1-2% organic revenue growth and EPS of $4.70 to $4.82. Cash flow in Q4 was $61.8 million and for fiscal 2025 was $251.5 million. Segment reviews showed growth in North American and International OTC Healthcare. Company outlook projects overcoming challenges for continued growth. Visit the Company's website for more details on their financials and products.
news.headline: Prestige Consumer Healthcare Inc. achieves record revenue and earnings in fiscal 2025 - $296.5M in Q4, $1,137.8M in total. EPS of $4.29, reduced leverage to 2.4x. Forecasting 1%-2% organic revenue growth and $4.70-$4.82 EPS for fiscal 2026.

news.summary: Crawford United Corporation reported strong financial results for the quarter ended March 31, 2025. Sales amounted to $43.3 million, a 12.7% increase from the same period in 2024. Operating income rose to $4.9 million, showing a 7.0% increase. Net income was $3.1 million, or $0.88 per fully diluted share, up 4.5% from the first quarter of 2024. EBITDA As Defined increased slightly to $6.8 million. The company's President and CEO, Brian Powers, expressed confidence in achieving long-term strategic goals and highlighted surpassing prior quarterly sales records. Crawford United Corporation serves diverse markets with specialized industrial products and operates in segments such as Commercial Air Handling Equipment and Industrial & Transportation Products. The company is positioned for growth and profitability through increased revenue opportunities and potential acquisitions.
news.headline: Crawford United Corp reported $43.3M sales in Q1 2025, up $5.9M from last quarter. Net income of $3.1M & EPS $0.88.

news.summary: Gogoro Inc., a global technology leader in battery swapping ecosystems, reported its financial results for Q1 2025. The revenue was $63.6 million, a decrease of 8.7% YoY. Battery swapping service revenue was $34.5 million, up 6.2% YoY. Hardware and others revenue was $29.1 million, down 21.8% YoY. Gross margin was 4.9%, down from 6.4% YoY. Net loss was $18.6 million compared to $13.1 million last year. Adjusted EBITDA was $14.3 million, up from $10.2 million. Gogoro is focusing on operational efficiency, cost reduction, and profitability milestones. They expect their battery swapping business to reach profitability in 2026 and hardware sales business in 2028. The company aims to save $25 million in 2025 through cost efficiency plans. They reiterated a revenue forecast of $295 million to $315 million for 2025, with emphasis on the Taiwan market. Gogoro plans to complete their ongoing battery upgrade initiatives by the end of 2025, impacting their gross margin in the short term. Gogoro expects to have sufficient funding to meet near-term growth objectives. Through optimization efforts and strategic plans, Gogoro remains committed to sustainable growth and creating long-term shareholder value.
news.headline: Gogoro's Q1 2025 results: $63.6M revenue, battery service rev up 6.2% to $34.5M, net loss $18.6M, adjusted EBITDA $14.3M. Focus on profitability milestones and cost reduction, with $25M savings expected in 2025. SWOT ongoing and gross margin impacted temporarily due to battery upgrades. Company plans for non-IFRS profitability by 2026 and 2028 in respective segments. Taiwan market forecasted stable, full-year revenue forecast between $295M to $315M, with significant focus on operational and cost efficiencies. Aiming for long-term growth and shareholder value creation.

news.summary: Home buyers purchasing newly constructed homes are benefitting from reduced mortgage rates, with rates dropping by approximately 0.5%. Additionally, the median list prices for new construction properties have decreased, resulting in a narrower price difference compared to existing homes, the smallest margin seen since 2020. In response to market demands, builders in 26 out of the top 100 U.S. metropolitan areas have begun offering smaller and more affordable homes. These trends are particularly noticeable in cities like Austin.
news.headline: New construction boosts affordability with lower mortgage rates and prices, reducing price gap with existing homes. Smaller, cheaper homes delivered in 26 top U.S. metros, but tariffs may hinder progress.

news.summary: Solidion Technology, Inc. revealed on May 8, 2025, that their patented platform technology can transform existing lithium-ion plants into solid-state battery manufacturers. The company, listed as NASDAQ:STI, is known for its advanced battery solutions. This innovative approach could potentially revolutionize the industry by leveraging current infrastructure for future battery production.
news.headline: Solidion plans to revolutionize lithium-ion battery industry by converting current facilities to solid-state producers.

news.summary: In Boston on May 8, 2025, the IEC Group acknowledged G-P (Globalization Partners) for its advanced technology and dedication to becoming the top global EOR provider. G-P, a remote-first company, was hailed as the foremost global employment leader in the industry.
news.headline: G-P (Globalization Partners) tops IEC Dynamic Map and named global EOR leader for third year.

news.summary: This year, Vienna, Va. saw remarkable contributions in the realm of employment for individuals with disabilities. Shockingly, more than 63% of working-age individuals with disabilities remain unemployed. The awards highlighted the significant efforts made by individuals, nonprofits, and businesses in addressing this issue. SourceAmerica®, serving as an authorized enterprise of AbilityOne®, played a central role in this initiative. The event celebrated transformative contributions aiming to improve the employment prospects of individuals with disabilities, shedding light on the urgency of the situation and the need for collective action.
news.headline: SourceAmerica recognizes leaders in disability employment at 2025 Xforce Conference, featuring 63% unemployment rate.

news.summary: Marengo Therapeutics, Inc. has announced the commencement of the STARt-002 clinical trial. This Phase 1b/2 study aims to evaluate the efficacy of invikafusp alfa when combined with Gilead's Trodelvy® in treating HR+/HER2- and TNBC breast cancer patients. The trial will involve patients from prominent breast cancer centers in the United States and Canada. The study officially began in Cambridge, Massachusetts on May 8, 2025. This collaborative research endeavor is set to engage a significant number of participants and healthcare professionals in the oncology field, reflecting the company's dedication to advancing breast cancer treatment.
news.headline: Marengo initiates STARt-002 trial with dual T cell agonist Invikafusp Alfa with Trodelvy® for breast cancer patients.
